nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Polish, Slovenian, Czech, Sorbian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Slavic brus ‘whetstone’, presumably a metonymic occupational name for someone who honed or sharpened blades; possibly also a nickname for a clumsy or coarse fellow. Compare Bruss .
Slovenian: from an old dialect short form of the personal name Ambrož (see Ambrose ). Compare Bruss .
French: topographic name for someone living on a heath, from Occitan brusc ‘heather’, or a habitational name from (Le/Les) Brus, the name of several places in southern France.

variants.name: Bruss
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Eldor, Reinhard, Rudie, Wilhelm.
German (also Brüss): from a short form of the medieval personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).
Americanized or Germanized form of Polish, Slovenian, Czech, Sorbian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic)   Brus  , meaning ‘whetstone’ and, as a Slovenian name, also ‘Ambrose’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus was born in Yorkshire, England as the daughter of Sir Peter de Brus II and Helewisa de Lancaster. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Robert de Ros of Wark II. She died before 30 January 1307, in Kendal, Westmorland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus est née à Everwyck, Angleterre, fille de Sir Peter de Brus II et Helewisa de Lancaster. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Robert de Ros of Wark II. Elle est décédée avant 30 janvier 1307, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus wurde in Yorkshire, England, als Tochter von Sir Peter de Brus II und Helewisa de Lancaster geboren. Sie hatte mit Robert de Ros of Wark II mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 30. Januar 1307 in Kendal, Westmorland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus è nata il nel Yorkshire, England come figlia di Sir Peter de Brus II e Helewisa de Lancaster. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Robert de Ros of Wark II. Morì prima del 30 gennaio 1307, nel Kendal, Westmorland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de BrusはイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir Peter de Brus IIとHelewisa de Lancasterの娘として生まれた。彼女はRobert de Ros of Wark IIとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1307年1月30日以前にイギリスウエストモーランドケンダルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus(은)는 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir Peter de Brus II(와)과 Helewisa de Lancaster의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert de Ros of Wark II(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1307년 1월 30일 이전 영국 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus nasceu em Yorkshire, Inglaterra filha de Sir Peter de Brus II e Helewisa de Lancaster. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Robert de Ros of Wark II. Ela faleceu antes 30 de janeiro de 1307, em Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus родилась в местности еркшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Sir Peter de Brus II и Helewisa de Lancaster. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Robert de Ros of Wark II. Она умерла перед 30 январь 1307 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brus nació en Yorkshire, Inglaterra como hija de Sir Peter de Brus II y Helewisa de Lancaster. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Robert de Ros of Wark II. Murió antes de 30 de enero de 1307, en Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra.
